channel.title: Dental Blog For Towne Lake Family Dentistry | Dental Blog | Page 1

item.title: How Routine Teeth Cleanings Prevent Permanent Bone Loss Around Your Teeth
item.description: Routine teeth cleanings do more than keep your smile looking fresh. They help protect the bone that supports your teeth. At Towne Lake Family Dentistry in Woodstock, GA, we use preventive dental care to help patients lower their risk of gum disease, tooth loss, and long-term oral health problems...
item.content:encoded:
Routine teeth cleanings do more than keep your smile looking fresh. They help protect the bone that supports your teeth. At Towne Lake Family Dentistry in Woodstock, GA, we use preventive dental care to help patients lower their risk of gum disease, tooth loss, and long-term oral health problems.
 

When plaque and tartar build up along the gumline, bacteria can irritate the gums and lead to inflammation. If this is not treated, the infection can move deeper below the gums and begin damaging the bone around the teeth. Once bone loss happens, it cannot grow back on its own, which is why regular dental cleanings matter.
 

Why Bone Loss Happens Around Teeth

The bone around your teeth acts like a foundation. It keeps your teeth stable and helps maintain your bite. Gum disease is one of the most common reasons this bone begins to break down.
 

In its early stage, gum disease may cause redness, swelling, or bleeding when brushing and flossing. As it progresses, pockets can form between the teeth and gums. These pockets collect bacteria and make it easier for infection to reach the supporting bone.
 

Many patients in Woodstock do not notice bone loss right away because it can happen slowly. By the time teeth feel loose or gums begin to recede, the damage may already be advanced.
 

How Teeth Cleanings Help Prevent Bone Loss

A routine teeth cleaning removes plaque and tartar that brushing and flossing cannot fully clear at home. Tartar is hardened buildup that can only be removed with professional dental tools. When tartar is left in place, it gives bacteria more space to grow and irritate the gums.
 

During a dental cleaning, we also check for early signs of gum disease. This gives us the chance to recommend treatment before the infection affects the bone around your teeth. Preventive visits help us catch small concerns before they become bigger, more expensive, and harder to treat.
 

Signs You May Need A Dental Cleaning

Even if your teeth feel fine, routine cleanings are still important. However, you should schedule a visit if you notice:

  • Gums that bleed when brushing or flossing
  • Red, swollen, or tender gums
  • Bad breath that does not go away
  • Gum recession or teeth that look longer
  • Loose teeth or changes in your bite
  • Tartar buildup near the gumline
 

These symptoms may point to gum inflammation or periodontal disease. The sooner we evaluate your gums, the better we can help protect your teeth and bone health.
 

Cleanings Support Long-Term Oral Health

Routine dental cleanings are one of the simplest ways to protect your smile. They help reduce harmful bacteria, support healthier gums, and lower the risk of permanent bone loss around your teeth.
 

For many patients, cleanings every six months are recommended. Some patients with gum disease or a history of periodontal problems may need more frequent visits. At our Woodstock dental office, we can help determine the right schedule based on your oral health needs.
 

Preventive Dental Care In Woodstock, GA

At Towne Lake Family Dentistry, we believe prevention is the best way to protect your natural teeth. Our team provides dental exams and teeth cleanings in Woodstock, GA, with a focus on keeping your gums, teeth, and supporting bone as healthy as possible.
 

To schedule your next routine teeth cleaning, contact Towne Lake Family Dentistry in Woodstock, GA by calling (770) 525-8581.

item.title: How to Care for Your Tooth After a Root Canal
item.description: A root canal is designed to remove infected or damaged tissue inside the tooth, relieve discomfort, and help preserve your natural smile. While the treatment itself is important, how you care for your tooth afterward also plays a major role in healing and long-term success.
item.content:encoded:
Why Aftercare Matters After a Root Canal

A root canal is designed to remove infected or damaged tissue inside the tooth, relieve discomfort, and help preserve your natural smile. While the treatment itself is important, how you care for your tooth afterward also plays a major role in healing and long-term success.
 

At Towne Lake Family Dentistry, we guide our patients through each step so they know what to expect after endodontic treatment and how to protect the treated tooth.
 

What to Expect After Treatment

It is normal to have some tenderness after a root canal, especially when chewing or touching the area. This usually improves as the surrounding tissue heals. If we place a temporary filling or restoration, the tooth may feel slightly different until the final restoration is completed.
 

Mild soreness can often be managed with the instructions we provide after your visit. However, worsening pain, swelling, or a bite that feels uneven should be checked by our dental team.
 

Be Careful While Numb

Your mouth may stay numb for several hours after your appointment. During this time, avoid chewing until feeling returns. This helps prevent accidentally biting your cheek, tongue, or lip.
 

Once the numbness wears off, try to chew on the opposite side of your mouth if the treated tooth does not yet have its final crown or restoration.
 

Keep the Area Clean

Good oral hygiene is still important after a root canal. Brush gently around the treated tooth and continue flossing unless we give you different instructions. Keeping the area clean helps reduce bacteria and supports the healing process.
 

A treated tooth still needs the same daily care as your other teeth. Root canal therapy removes the infected tissue inside the tooth, but the outside of the tooth can still develop decay or gum problems without proper care.
 

Protect the Tooth Until It Is Restored

In many cases, a tooth that has had a root canal needs a crown to help protect it from cracking or breaking. This is especially common for back teeth, which handle more chewing pressure.
 

Until your final restoration is placed, avoid foods that could damage the tooth, including:

  • Hard candies, ice, or very crunchy snacks
  • Sticky foods that could pull at a temporary filling
  • Chewing directly on the treated tooth
  • Using your teeth to open packages or bite hard objects
 

Watch for Changes

Most patients recover smoothly after a root canal, but it is important to pay attention to how the tooth feels. Contact us if you notice swelling, severe discomfort, sensitivity that does not improve, or if your temporary filling feels loose or falls out.
 

We would rather check the tooth early than allow a small issue to become more complicated.
 

Stay on Track With Follow-Up Care

Root canal aftercare does not end once the discomfort improves. Completing your follow-up care and final restoration helps seal and protect the tooth. Skipping this step can leave the tooth vulnerable to reinfection, fracture, or future dental problems.
 

Our goal is to help you keep your natural tooth functioning comfortably for years to come.
 

For root canal treatment and follow-up care in Woodstock, GA, contact Towne Lake Family Dentistry at 120 North Medical Parkway, Building 200 Suite #100, Woodstock, GA 30189 by calling (770) 525-8581.

item.title: How TempSure Envi Helps Reduce Fine Lines and Wrinkles
item.description: Fine lines and wrinkles are a natural part of aging, but that does not mean you have to feel stuck with changes in your skin. At Towne Lake Family Dentistry, we offer TempSure Envi as a non-surgical facial aesthetics option for patients who want smoother, tighter-looking skin without a more inva...
item.content:encoded:
Fine lines and wrinkles are a natural part of aging, but that does not mean you have to feel stuck with changes in your skin. At Towne Lake Family Dentistry, we offer TempSure Envi as a non-surgical facial aesthetics option for patients who want smoother, tighter-looking skin without a more invasive procedure.
 

TempSure Envi uses radiofrequency technology to gently warm the skin, helping support natural collagen production and improve the appearance of fine lines and wrinkles over time.
 

How TempSure Envi Works

TempSure Envi delivers controlled radiofrequency energy into the deeper layers of the skin. This heat helps stimulate the body’s natural healing response, which can encourage collagen renewal.
 

Collagen is an important protein that helps skin look firm, smooth, and elastic. As collagen production slows with age, the skin may begin to show fine lines, wrinkles, and mild laxity. TempSure Envi helps address these changes by supporting healthier skin structure from within.
 

What Areas Can TempSure Envi Treat?

TempSure Envi can be used on several areas where fine lines, wrinkles, and skin laxity commonly appear. Treatment is often chosen for areas such as:

  • Forehead lines
  • Crow’s feet around the eyes
  • Smile lines
  • Fine lines around the mouth
  • Loose skin along the jawline
  • Mild creasing on the lower face
 

Because the treatment is customizable, we can focus on the areas that matter most to your goals.
 

Why Patients Choose TempSure Envi

Many patients are interested in facial rejuvenation but are not ready for surgery, injections, or extended downtime. TempSure Envi offers a comfortable option that fits into a busy schedule.
 

The treatment is designed to be gentle, and many patients describe the sensation as a warm massage. There is typically little to no downtime, which means you can return to your normal routine soon after your appointment.
 

Natural-Looking Results Over Time

TempSure Envi is not meant to dramatically change the way you look. Instead, it helps refresh the skin gradually by supporting collagen remodeling. Results can vary from patient to patient, but the goal is smoother, firmer, more youthful-looking skin that still looks like you.
 

Some patients may notice improvement after treatment, while collagen-related changes often continue to develop over the following weeks.
 

Pairing Facial Aesthetics With Personalized Care

At Towne Lake Family Dentistry, we take a personalized approach to facial aesthetics. Before recommending TempSure Envi, we talk with you about your concerns, skin goals, and the areas you would like to improve.
 

This helps us create a treatment plan that feels natural, balanced, and appropriate for your needs. Whether you are starting to notice early fine lines or want to improve mild skin laxity, TempSure Envi may be a helpful option.
 

Refresh Your Skin With TempSure Envi In Woodstock, GA

If you are interested in reducing fine lines and wrinkles with a non-surgical treatment, contact Towne Lake Family Dentistry in Woodstock, GA by calling (770) 525-8581.

item.title: How Long Do Dental Fillings Last?
item.description: The lifespan of a filling depends on the material used, where the filling is placed, your bite pressure, and how well the tooth is cared for over time. In our Woodstock office, we often remind patients that a filling can last for many years, but regular exams are still important so we can catch ...
item.content:encoded:
Dental fillings are designed to restore a tooth after decay or minor damage, but they do not last forever. The lifespan of a filling depends on the material used, where the filling is placed, your bite pressure, and how well the tooth is cared for over time. In our Woodstock office, we often remind patients that a filling can last for many years, but regular exams are still important so we can catch wear before it turns into a larger problem.
 

The Average Lifespan Of A Dental Filling

Different materials tend to last for different lengths of time. Composite fillings, which are tooth-colored and commonly used for visible areas, often last several years with proper care. Other materials, such as amalgam, may last longer in some cases. Even so, there is no exact expiration date. A small filling on a front tooth may hold up differently than a larger filling on a molar that handles more chewing force.
 

What Affects How Long Fillings Last?

Several factors can shorten or extend the life of a dental filling:

  • The size of the filling
  • The location of the tooth
  • Teeth grinding or clenching
  • Daily brushing and flossing habits
  • Diet, especially frequent sugary or acidic foods
  • How often you come in for routine dental visits

When a filling is placed on a tooth that takes heavy pressure, it may wear down faster than one in a less active area of the mouth.
 

Signs A Filling May Need To Be Replaced

A filling can look fine on the surface while beginning to fail around the edges. That is why routine checkups matter. Some common signs that a filling may need attention include sensitivity to hot or cold, pain when biting, a rough edge you can feel with your tongue, or a crack in the tooth. In some cases, decay can form around an older filling, which means the tooth may need additional treatment.
 

Good Habits Can Help Fillings Last Longer

One of the best ways to protect your fillings is to care for the surrounding tooth structure. Brushing twice a day, flossing daily, and keeping up with professional cleanings can make a real difference. If you grind your teeth at night, a custom night guard may also help reduce stress on both your fillings and your natural teeth.
 

Why Regular Exams Matter

Dental fillings do not usually fail all at once. In many cases, they wear down gradually. During routine exams, we check for cracks, leakage, decay, and bite changes that may affect the filling. Catching those issues early may help you avoid a more complex procedure later, such as a crown or root canal.
 

If you have an older filling or have noticed sensitivity when chewing, it may be time to have it evaluated. Contact Towne Lake Family Dentistry in Woodstock, GA by calling (770) 525-8581 or visit us at 120 North Medical Parkway, Building 200 Suite #100, Woodstock, GA 30189.

item.title: How VELscope Screening Detects Abnormal Tissue
item.description: At Towne Lake Family Dentistry in Woodstock, Georgia, we use VELscope® screening to help identify soft tissue changes that may not be visible during a standard visual exam. This technology gives us a closer look at the tissues in your mouth so we can detect abnormal areas early and recommend the...
item.content:encoded:
At Towne Lake Family Dentistry in Woodstock, Georgia, we use VELscope® screening to help identify soft tissue changes that may not be visible during a standard visual exam. This technology gives us a closer look at the tissues in your mouth so we can detect abnormal areas early and recommend the next steps when needed.

What VELscope Screening Looks For

Soft tissue lines many parts of the mouth, including the cheeks, gums, tongue, floor of the mouth, and palate. When this tissue changes in an unusual way, it can be a sign that something needs further attention. We use VELscope® screening to help identify areas that may not appear normal under ordinary light, giving us another way to evaluate your oral health during your visit.

How The Technology Works

VELscope® uses a specialized light to examine oral tissue. Healthy tissue and abnormal tissue can respond differently under that light, which helps unusual areas stand out more clearly. This does not automatically mean a serious condition is present, but it does give us more information than a visual exam alone. Some tissue changes can be very subtle, especially in the early stages, so this added level of screening can be valuable.

Why Early Detection Matters

When abnormal tissue is detected early, it is often easier to evaluate and manage. That is one of the biggest benefits of VELscope® screening. Rather than waiting for changes to become more noticeable, we can look for signs of concern sooner. Early detection supports better decision-making and can lead to faster follow-up care when necessary.

What VELscope May Help Identify

VELscope® screening may help us detect several types of tissue abnormalities, including:

  • Bacterial infections
  • Evidence of trauma
  • Dysplastic tissue
  • Oral cancer

This makes screening an important part of preventive care, especially because some oral health concerns may develop without obvious symptoms.

Who Should Consider Screening

VELscope® screening can be a smart addition to routine dental visits for many adults. We recommend regular screening for patients over 18, and those with higher risk factors may benefit from even closer monitoring during routine care. Even if you are not having discomfort, screening can still be worthwhile because abnormal tissue does not always cause pain or visible symptoms right away.

What To Expect at Your Visit

The screening process is quick and noninvasive. We use the VELscope® device to examine the soft tissues in your mouth and look for areas that appear different from the surrounding tissue. If we find something unusual, the next step may be monitoring the area more closely or recommending further evaluation. Our goal is to be thorough, proactive, and focused on protecting your long-term oral health.

Contact Towne Lake Family Dentistry in Woodstock, Georgia by calling (770) 525-8581 to schedule your dental exam and learn more about VELscope® screening.
